topics.name: Converging edges
topics.freshness: auto-rolled from the layers below — recomputed every build from the edge board
topics.lead: The sharpest draft edges aren't single facts — they're where two or three independent layers point the same way. These are the calls with the most support and the least to argue with. When the OL layer, the run-lean layer, and the RB-receiving layer all flag the same backfield, the price gap is real, not a one-source take. Below: 🎯 = the layers agree; ⚠ = they're cited on both sides, so judge it.

cases.name: Omarion Hampton
cases.find: Top-3 RB — lead back, 69.7% opportunity share (11th) when healthy; rookie ankle injuries "in the past" *(orig. premise "Harbaugh run-heavy" stale — Roman fired, new OC McDaniel)*
cases.act: Needs: Re-anchor claim to the McDaniel scheme; camp carry-share confirmation; the 69.7% opp-share figure unconfirmable *(health half corroborated 2026-06-03; mixed signal — held in Staging at 2026-06-04 sweep; 6/07: health fully corroborated — chargers.com/SI…

converge.line: 🎯 5 layers agree: OL, Run-lean, RB-receiving, Backfield, Coaching

cases.name: Denver backfield consolidation
cases.find: OL #1 + elite defense + only +1.6 PROE behind a 3-way committee (Harvey/Dobbins/Coleman) — whoever consolidates inherits fortress run volume at committee prices. FORK of the RJ Harvey fade row: fade Harvey at #74 while…

cases.name: Jahmyr Gibbs — floor
cases.find: Floor buy, not ceiling — league-wide biggest coaching-usage winner: Petzing run-lean install + Montgomery gone + good-D lead scripts lock a bellcow workload; consensus #5 prices the ceiling but under-prices the FLOOR/wo…

cases.name: Bucky Irving

cases.name: Jadarian Price — buy
cases.find: Buy at #81/RB28 — R1 capital + Shanahan-tree continuity (OC Fleury) + Elite-D / lead-friendly scripts + Charbonnet's ACL-PUP risk point to a lead role the rookie price doesn't reflect
cases.act: Needs: Resolve the 3-way camp battle (Holani/Wilson) the 6/07 diff flagged + Charbonnet return timeline; 2nd source on first-team carry share. *Surfaced by the 6/06 coaching pass; Hai's call to log 6/08*

cases.name: Ladd McConkey
cases.find: Beats-ADP buy — priced off the injury-wrecked WR30 2025 while everything rebounds at once: new OC McDaniel scheme, OL health (Slater/Alt), proj 25% target share as the alpha
cases.act: Needs: 2nd analyst ranking him top-15 WR / camp usage; monitor the 2025 calf-biceps-foot-ankle cluster *(6/07: OL-health premise corroborated — Slater/Alt ahead of schedule, June reporting via OL layer; the ranking signal is the open half)*

cases.name: Saquon Barkley
cases.find: Fade vs ADP — 2025 efficiency cratered (4.1 YPC from 5.8; career-low 1.6 YACo) AND A.J. Brown's box-lightening gravity left for NE → stacked boxes vs a brand-new under-center wide-zone (Mannion); market still RB6-8
cases.act: Don't pay the RB6-8 price; if I want PHI RB exposure it's at a discount or pass. Pairs with the CMC age/decline fade theme. *Refresh 2026-06-07: counter-evidence — OL topic layer ranks PHI #2/32 (all 5 starters back) and run-lean profiles PHI as a fortress wi…

topics.name: Offensive line
topics.freshness: anchors fetched 2026-06-07; FTN rankings are post-2026-draft (updates again "before heavy fantasy draft season"); PFSN OLi is final 2025-season grades (Apr 2026). Volatile fields (injury, position battles) need a refresh at camp open (~late July).
topics.lead: The projected 1–32 board is a continuity bet: FTN's post-draft rank vs PFSN's 2025 grade, and the gap between them is the bet. DEN #1 (all five back, 5 sacks on 717 pass-pro snaps). Biggest risers PHI and ATL; biggest fallers BAL, CIN, MIA. The fantasy use isn't the rank itself — it's where a line move quietly changes a back's or QB's floor.

topics.name: Run-lean / script
topics.freshness: 2025 full-season splits fetched 2026-06-07; defense/OL/backfield inputs from same-day layers. MAJOR CAVEAT — 21 teams have new OCs for 2026 (ETR), so 2025 tendencies are weakened league-wide; trend arrows capture announced shifts. Refresh after preseason.
topics.lead: "Will they run?" = three multiplied layers: scheme intent (PROE, what coaches choose), script tailwind (defense + OL — do they get the leads?), and volume clarity (who collects the carries). Read the intent-vs-result gap: BAL/SEA are run-heavy in both (real identity); ARI threw most but only because they trailed all year (forced). The fantasy edge is the run fortress: intent + tailwind + clarity all aligned.

topics.name: RB receiving (PPR)
topics.freshness: fetched 2026-06-07. 2025-season stats are final; QB target-rate table is 2022–24 data (apply the rate to the QB, not his old team). Re-check triggers — Fantasy Points 2026 positional-tendencies edition (~late July), PFF "Captain Checkdown" 2025-season edition, camp 3rd-down role reports.
topics.refresh: late July (FP tendencies 2026 ed. + PFF Captain Checkdown 2025 ed.) + camp 3rd-down roles
topics.lead: PPR-critical. The checkdown is now schemed, not a bailout — two-high shells on passing downs jumped 44% (2019) → 63% (2024), and RBs took 60%+ of every checkdown category PFF charted. A target ≈ 2.5× a carry in PPR expected-points. This is the tiebreaker that splits weekly starters from matchup plays in the committee-soup RB middle. Follow the QB's tendency, not the team.

cases.act: Needs: IND QB1 resolution at camp (Jones vs Richardson first-team reps)

teams.line: elite — Bijan Robinson · 103 tgt; top-5 routes/tgt/rec all 3 pro seasons

elite — CMC · 129 tgt/22.5% share, led RBs in every receiving category + 91.7 PFF recv; age-30 share watch

strong — Chase Brown · 88 tgt, 5th in rec two straight yrs; Perine trims pass downs

moderate — Travis Etienne · 52 tgt; Kamara 3.5/gm in 11 gms if he stays — Kubiak-era usage memory
paradox — Cam Skattebo · 4.0 tgt/gm (CMC-tier) BUT 11.6 pass-block dead-last + Nagy negative; Tracy 47 tgt insurance
quality-over-volume — Josh Jacobs · 89.5 PFF recv (career-high, real conversion) on modest 44 tgt; suspension risk rides all of it
rebound watch — Breece Hall · career-low 10% share 2025 vs top-4 RB receiver 23-24; Clay flags bounce

specialist-driven — Aaron Jones · trusted vet pass-downs; Mason early downs only
specialist-driven — Rachaad White · 89.1% career catch rate = best in NFL history (100+ tgt); JCM no pass downs

specialist-driven — LeQuint Allen · designated passing-down back; Tuten partial

specialist-driven — Kenneth Gainwell · 85 tgt (RB6) signed for the role; Irving primary if shoulder/role holds
split — Chuba Hubbard · primary pass-down back; Brooks the health wildcard
split tension — Henderson vs Stevenson · Henderson 83% catch upside vs Stevenson 9.1 Y/T + 69.9 pass-pro incumbency
target-rich split — Harvey/Coleman/Badie · Nix 19% RB rate; Harvey 58 tgt, Coleman 3-down rookie, Badie best pass-pro (80.9)
unknown — Jadarian Price · rookie receiving profile UNVERIFIED (gap); Charbonnet career-low 24 tgt; Fleury Shanahan-tree continuity
volume w/o juice — Javonte Williams · 51 tgt but 2.7 Y/T — LAST among qualified RBs
QB-dependent — Jonathan Taylor · top-10 routes/tgt 2025 BUT 1.3 tgt/gm w/ Richardson vs 3.6 without — QB battle = PPR ceiling
low — Kyren Williams · Stafford 11% RB rate starves the backfield despite McVay design; Kyren never >36 catches
low — James Cook · 40 tgt (26th) = his ceiling-limiter; "unleash" lever unproven
low + exception watch — Woody Marks · offense historically ignores RB receiving; Marks (24-208-3 rookie) the 3rd-down exception
low + wrinkles — Kenneth Walker · 36 tgt; Mahomes aDOT >8.0 again; satellite B. Smith; Fields-early = temporary RB-tgt spike (19.2%)
near-zero — Justice Hill · Henry 21 tgt/outside top-45; Lamar 14% + Rush 12% — don't chase BAL RB receiving
scheme-low/QB-high — Jaylen Warren · McCarthy doesn't design RB routes BUT Rodgers = fastest TTT (2.59s) + bottom-4 aDOT; Warren 89% catch + #2 pass-block = stealth PPR floor

topics.name: Backfield — bell cow vs committee
topics.freshness: per-team carry shares are the profiles' who-eats layer (mostly 2026-06-03, diff-updated through 6/07); league-trend sources fetched 2026-06-07. Camp battles volatile — refresh classifications at camp (~late July) and final cutdowns.
topics.lead: True bell cows are structurally scarcer than the 16-game era but the decline has stopped (~4.8/yr since 2021, stabilizing). Volume still concentrates at the very top — four backs topped 300 carries in 2025. The market consequence: the RB25–36 tier is committee soup, so clear workloads are concentrated in the first ~2 rounds and evaporate fast. Pay up for clarity or punt to the receiving backs.

topics.name: Defense units
topics.freshness: anchors fetched 2026-06-07. KNOWN GAP — no true 1–32 secondary ranking exists yet for 2026 (Sharp/PFF publish ~late July); secondary tiers below are a synthesis and marked accordingly. B/R's 1–32 overall ranking PRE-dates the 6/1 Garrett trade (adjusted inline).
topics.lead: Posture is the lens: Aggressive (blitz DNA, boom-bust), Disguise (sim-pressure, Fangio/Macdonald tree), Solid (rush-four, keep it in front). The two biggest YoY swings are both in the secondary — LAR 26th → top-5 (McDuffie+Watson heist from KC) and KC gutted (both starting CBs gone). Defense feeds fantasy two ways: D/ST streaming and the script tailwind that decides whether an offense gets to run.

topics.name: Coaching changes
topics.lead: Coaching turnover is where ADP is most likely wrong — the field prices last year's scheme, so the more a staff changed, the more its usage sits mis-priced. Full resets are the edge: NYG (Harbaugh), MIA (Hafley), and ATL (Stefanski) all install run-first, compressing the pass game (Nabers, London/Pitts ceilings down) while feeding the lead back (Skattebo, Achane, Bijan). The sharper tell is a new-OC install that corroborates a live board edge — Reich's pass-volume jump in NYJ behind Garrett Wilson, Petzing's run-lean making Gibbs the biggest usage winner in football, Zac Robinson's McVay tree lifting Egbuka — the calls with both the scheme logic and the edge board behind them.
